input.instructions: Implement the core logic to identify the hidden polyominoes. Given the set of possible polyomino shapes and the noisy feedback from the I/O handler, develop a strategy to minimize probing costs. This should involve a search or probabilistic model to estimate the most likely locations and orientations of the oil fields. The engine should decide which coordinates to query next based on information gain or cost-efficiency and finally output the most probable set of oil cells. Focus on handling the noise parameter provided in the initial input.

run_level_defaults.selected_prompt_text:
Break the coding task into a small number of functional implementation components for other agents. 

For interactive programming tasks, ensure helpers focus on distinct architectural needs, such as the interaction protocol/I/O loop and the core algorithmic strategy or state management. 

Limit the number of helpers to a maximum of 2. Each helper must receive one focused, non-overlapping task. Use plain text only inside helper instructions; do not use markdown, code fences, or LaTeX.

CRITICAL: Your entire response must be a single, valid JSON object. Do not include any text, markdown formatting, or code fences before or after the JSON object. Your response must start with { and end with }.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "delegate",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "helpers": [
    {
      "label": "helper_1",
      "instructions": "plain-text subproblem for the first helper"
    }
  ],
  "synthesis_brief": "how the parent should combine the child results"
}
run_level_defaults.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}
